reviews.review_description: Amazing game, very polished in every way and probably the best RTS 1v1 experience out there by a mile. However, matchmaking is filled with bots. Bots don't interact with you in any way except by staring at you and are incredibly bad. This is a very disgusting practice of misleading users by matching them with bots. The most infuriating thing is that if the matchmaking takes more than 1 min then you get matched with a bot, potentially losing the ability to get matched with a real human because you are playing vs a bot.

reviews.review_title: So accessible, so much fun, and so CUTE
reviews.review_description:
First impressions gameplay here (remove the space before the "be"):

https://youtu. be/qsBpVHX_tTU?si=VBmBT3YpKvl8cwr0

Oh man, those animals wearing their little armour sets are so adorable! I'm not normally good at this kind of RTS game, but I found it very accessible and easy to control. Also, I won my first match!! Very satisfying.

reviews.review_title: Best Moba on VR to Date:
reviews.review_description:
If you are looking for a Polished Moba that looks and feels great, this is the game for you. Not only is it a Moba, but it also includes the wonderous world of Moss and all of its inhabitants. 

Here are some + points:
-Animations are smooth and fun
-Graphics are of top quality
-Sound design fits the world of Moss
-Great movement options
-Diverse cast of champions
-Simple to learn gameplay
-Objectives for the game are clear to define

I hope you all try out Glassbreakers and give it a shot!

So played glass breakers for a few days now and great graphics well thought out and a nice little time killer however ......
Firstly the match making is extremely unfair my third match in I had characters who were level 3 going against Characters who were level 23 ! 
Learning the ropes and pitched against someone who has clearly played a lot is very unfair and very off putting ! 
Secondly Characters, cosmetics and gems are hidden behind paywalls to get stronger and better heroes if you don't pay is an extreme grind!

I have already played several rounds as a playtester and find the game very well implemented. Fans of tabletop games will certainly not be disappointed. The visual and auditory elements fit and create a typical Moss atmosphere. However, I hope for more and more sophisticated maps, perhaps with topography for tactical advantages such as attack range and movement speeds.

Hard to review this one. The visuals are fantastic and if you played moss you will love the Easter eggs but this is not a board game like it is touting to be. Yes it is on a board, but that’s it. This game os a RTS with a rouge like upgrading system. 

Every game I played you have three warriors who need to go to places on the map to increase their stats then you attack the other players base.  The other player is doing the same thing. Whoever can destroy the base first wins.  That’s it.  No strategy.  

Multiplayer is only if you have a friend and they have a code. Finding a random game is always the A.I. 

Devs- 

I like the game but make it more like a board game. Maybe make it turn based, dice. The hexes need to have meaning, like brush, fire, water, trees, ect to make one want to use them for defensive or offensive purposes.

Thank the lord for sidequest or otherwise, I wouldn't have found this. Great start so far, however still needs a lot of work. Wish I could control camera with analog sticks.  Would apperciate if devs added more PVE content into the game as well such as a continuous single player campaign or Coop vs AI bots. Also, to change the background scenery from time to time during matches.

The level of polish is there, unique gameplay among quest library, free to play with season pass and option to pay for faster class unlocks and cosmetics. Cannot buy anything granting you extra power. Great early release, let’s see if devs can keep up releasing new champions, new maps possibly etc. Try it out, it’s worth the time!

Yrab





Love it

reviews.review_title: Help I don’t know where to put the code
reviews.review_description: Can someone help me I’ve been trying to find out where to put the code in

it's not bad, it's just perfect. It's hard to move around and everything. But anyways it's a pretty fun game. I play with friends and stuff rarely do I eve to play with random people who, don't show up and.  They are just Too competitive
It’s mad fun and has a high skill cap and it’s not pay to win and one of the characters makes some racial remarks at rats so ❤️❤️

genuinely entertaining and I spent a decent amount of time playing the matches. It’s not overly complicated, the tutorials are easy to grasp and if you’re familiar with fantasy role-playing games you’ll pick this up quick and enjoy it. I’ve read reviews saying it’s too easy, but I think it’s just a case of the creators delivering what they advertise. It’s not that it’s easy to the point of being boring, it’s that the game is simple and the creators knew they weren’t trying to reinvent the wheel making it. You might not play this every day but it will most definitely entertain you for a few hours.

After playing until my headset died for a few days in a row, I am spellbound by Glassbreakers. The polish is much greater than anything else I've played on the quest so far, far better than what is expected of a free, early access game. 

    If I had to explain how it feels, I'd say it's just like figurine playing when you are a kid. The "champion" mice respond to your commands, and look outworld-ishly cute while doing so. What happens after is of books and imagination.
    It's joy to look around yourself, and find the fairy tale-like environment built here (really, the designer for that needs a raise). 
    I do believe though this is a bit miscategorized. I'd say it's more RTS with some MOBA DNA infused in it. The most important thing here is strategy, not team play as... Well, you are the team! 
    Lastly, it is worrying how the "free" in-game currency will be handled. For now, in the 3 days I played I already afford a new character. This is really good, but is that guaranteed to be the same with all season passes? Only time will tell.

    Everyone and their mother ( if they own a quest ) should at least give Glassbreakers a try. All the nuts who like both RTS and MOBAs should know THIS is the game for them. At least I know it is for me.

First off, the graphics and animations are phenomenal! The gameplay does take a bit to get used to but nothing too difficult.
The problem however is that so far, I am only able to get to the end of the tutorial. Once it's completed, the game freezes on a black screen with no loading bar or any indication that it's loading anything.
If this issue improves I will gladly add more stars.
EDIT: I have been able to progress more into the game, and I have had a better experience  with Glassbreakers since my initial install.
Graphics are still solid as well as gameplay. However I feel the matchmaking could be better as a newer player with only the basic units I was paired repeatedly with players that had epic class units.
But overall, a decent game.
I’m naming my champion Despereaux 🗡️🐁

it's a very interesting game concept and they really got the mechanics down perfectly and it runs smoothly and it feels really fun to play though i do wish there was a easier way to earn the resources to purchase different units other than paying money for them

Like other opinions says, it's a shame. It had the potential to be a great game, turn based, with strategy and a good evolution of the different characters, but it's only a fair game, visually fantastic, but nothing more. It doesn't represent a challenge.

Glassbreakers is an engaging, immaculately themed 1v1 dueling game where each person controls a team of 3 heros trying to destroy the opponent’s glass.

Pros:
- Art and theming is beautiful and on point for the Moss universe.
- There’s more content than a lot of other free games that have recently released.
- The progression/currency system is nice; there are micro-transactions but everything can be earned through just playing the game.
- The actual gameplay and matches feel really good and have a high skill cap.
 
Cons: 
- There aren’t a lot of players online so sometimes to end up just playing bots
- There are sometimes glitches that force you to restart the game to continue playing, such as getting stuck on a black screen.

Overall I would give Glassbreakers a try, especially if you’ve played the Moss games. Also, I would recommend joining the official Discord as you can use it to find matches and interact with the devs. The game is in its infant stages right now, and I’m looking forward to seeing what’s next!

I like the game, other people say that you get bots, and that is true and sometimes its a bit to easy. But the game is in its early stages and does not have many players therefore you have to wait long or there is a massieve skill isue. So personally i get that you are playing bots. Overall i think its a great game and wil give it 5 stars.
